Scan the options for one ending in 5: (a) 67000 ends 0; (b) 69375 ends 5 ✓; (c) 76500 ends 0; (d) 77200 ends 0.

Use the identity (a+b)² − 4ab = (a−b)². Here a−b = 957−932 = 25, so the answer is 25².
Units digit of 25 is 5, and 5² always ends in 5 (5×5=25). So the answer must end in 5.
Check options: (a) 576 ends 6; (b) 676 ends 6; (c) 529 ends 9; (d) 625 ends 5 ✓.
Only (d) ends in 5 — confirmed instantly by the units-digit rule for numbers ending in 5.
set1.sol2:
(a+b)² - 4ab = a² + 2ab + b² - 4ab = a² - 2ab + b² = (a-b)².
a - b = 957 - 932 = 25 → 25² = 625 → option (d).

set1.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:

The exact value works out to 2958 (see Solution 2), ending in 8 — only option (c) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

Boys = 34 × 87 = 2958 → option (c).

set1.en: A logistics company charges ₹15 per km for the first 80 km and ₹12.5 per km thereafter. A consignment was transported for a total of 132.5 km and the total cost was split in the ratio 2 : 3 between the sender and receiver. What amount did the receiver pay?
set1.hi: एक लॉजिस्टिक्स कंपनी पहले 80 किमी के लिए ₹15 प्रति किमी और उसके बाद ₹12.5 प्रति किमी का शुल्क लेती है। एक खेप को कुल 132.5 किमी तक ले जाया गया और कुल लागत को प्रेषक और प्राप्तकर्ता के बीच 2 : 3 के अनुपात में विभाजित किया गया। प्राप्तकर्ता ने कितनी राशि का भुगतान किया?
set1.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) ₹1200 → ends in 0; (b) ₹1350 → ends in 0; (c) ₹1444.2 → ends in 4; (d) ₹1113.75 → ends in 3.
The exact value works out to ₹1113.75 (see Solution 2), ending in 3 — only option (d) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set1.sol2:
Cost for first 80 km = 80×15 = ₹1200
Remaining distance = 132.5-80 = 52.5 km → cost = 52.5×12.5 = ₹656.25
Total cost = 1200+656.25 = ₹1856.25
Receiver's share (3 parts of 5) = 1856.25 × 3/5 = ₹1113.75 → option (d).

set2.en: Alok, Gita, and Suresh invest ₹56,000, ₹52,000, and ₹69,000 respectively to start a business. The units digit alone repeats across options here, so check the last TWO digits instead:
(a) ₹20,631 → last two digits 31; (b) ₹20,693 → last two digits 93; (c) ₹20,551 → last two digits 51; (d) ₹20,727 → last two digits 27.
The exact value is ₹20,631 (see Solution 2), whose last two digits are 31 — only option (a) matches, confirming it directly.
set2.sol2:

Remaining profit = 76700 - 23777 = ₹52,923
Ratio of investment = 56000:52000:69000 = 56:52:69 (sum = 177)
Suresh's share = 52923 × 69/177 = ₹20,631
This matches option (a) exactly by direct computation.

set2.sol2:

Cuboid formed: length l = 5a = 1110 cm, breadth b = 222 cm, height h = 222 cm
Lateral Surface Area = 2h(l+b) = 2×222×(1110+222) = 2×222×1332 = 5,91,408 cm² → option (a).

set2.en: The sum of the perimeter of two cubes, the length of whose edges are x cm and y cm, respectively, is 300 cm and the product of edges x and y is 156 cm². Find the sum of the volume of both cubes.

set2.sol1:

The exact value works out to 3925 cm³ (see Solution 2), ending in 5 — only option (d) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set2.sol2:
x+y=25, xy=156. Solving quadratic t²-25t+156=0 → t=13,12. So x=13, y=12 (or vice versa).
Sum of volumes = x³+y³ = 13³+12³ = 2197+1728 = 3925 cm³ → option (d).

set3.en: If x + y = 24 and x² + y² = 360, then find the value of (x⁴ + x²y² + y⁴).
set3.hi: यदि x + y = 24 और x² + y² = 360 है, तो (x⁴ + x²y² + y⁴) का मान ज्ञात कीजिए।
set3.sol1:
x⁴+x²y²+y⁴ = (x²+y²)² − (xy)². From x+y=24, x²+y²=360, we get 2xy = 24²−360 = 216, so xy=108.
Units digit shortcut: (x²+y²)² = 360² ends in 0 (since 360 ends in 0, any power of it ends in 0).
(xy)² = 108² — units digit of 108 is 8, and 8²=64, so 108² ends in 4.
Units digit of the answer = 0 − 4 → borrow gives units digit 6 (since ...00 − ...04 = ...96).
Check options: (a) 1,17,936 ends 6 ✓; (b) 1,25,878 ends 8; (c) 1,24,784 ends 4; (d) 2,15,869 ends 9.
Only (a) ends in 6 — confirmed.

Since 22+(−18)+(−4)=0, the identity a+b+c=0 ⟹ a³+b³+c³=3abc applies, so the answer is 3×22×(−18)×(−4) = 3×22×72.
Units digit shortcut: units digit of 22 is 2, of 72 is 2. 3×2×2 = 12 → units digit 2.
Check options: (a) 4800 ends 0; (b) 4819 ends 9; (c) 4752 ends 2 ✓; (d) 4617 ends 7.
Only (c) ends in 2 — confirmed without computing the full product.

Since 23−11−12=0, a+b+c=0 ⟹ a³+b³+c³=3abc, so the answer is 3×23×(−11)×(−12) = 3×23×132.
Units digit shortcut: units digit of 23 is 3, of 132 is 2. 3×3×2 = 18 → units digit 8.
Check options: (a) 9070 ends 0; (b) 8863 ends 3; (c) 9108 ends 8 ✓; (d) 9144 ends 4.
Only (c) ends in 8 — confirmed instantly.

Simplify the powers first: 3⁽⁵⁻²⁾ × 6⁽⁴⁻²⁾ × 4⁽³⁻¹⁾ = 3³ × 6² × 4² = 27 × 36 × 16.
Units digit shortcut: units digit of 27 is 7, of 36 is 6, of 16 is 6.
7×6 = 42 → keep 2; 2×6 = 12 → keep 2. Units digit of the answer = 2.
Check options: (a) 15558 ends 8; (b) 15554 ends 4; (c) 15552 ends 2 ✓; (d) 15559 ends 9.
Only (c) ends in 2 — confirmed.

The exact value works out to 78 (see Solution 2), ending in 8 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set4.en: The average of 12 numbers is 58. The average of the first 5 numbers is 55, and the average of the next 4 numbers is 62. If the 10th number is 10 less than the 11th number and 5 more than the 12th number, then what is the average of the 11th and 10th numbers?
set4.hi: 12 संख्याओं का औसत 58 है। पहली 5 संख्याओं का औसत 55 है, और अगली 4 संख्याओं का औसत 62 है। यदि 10वीं संख्या 11वीं संख्या से 10 कम है और 12वीं संख्या से 5 अधिक है, तो 11वीं और 10वीं संख्याओं का औसत कितना है?
set4.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 52 → ends in 2; (b) 61 → ends in 1; (c) 60 → ends in 0; (d) 58 → ends in 8.
The exact value works out to 61 (see Solution 2), ending in 1 — only option (b) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set4.en: A book priced at ₹600 is given two successive discounts of 10% and 20%. Find the final selling price.
set4.hi: ₹600 की एक किताब पर 10% और 20% की दो लगातार छूट दी गई हैं। अंतिम विक्रय मूल्य ज्ञात करें।
set4.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) ₹432 → ends in 2; (b) ₹396 → ends in 6; (c) ₹384 → ends in 4; (d) ₹350 → ends in 0.
The exact value works out to ₹432 (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set4.en: The average of 11 numbers is 61. The average of the first six numbers is 57 and of the last six is 65. What is the sixth number?
set4.hi: 11 संख्याओं का औसत 61 है। पहली छह संख्याओं का औसत 57 है और अंतिम छह का 65 है। छठी संख्या क्या है?
set4.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 62 → ends in 2; (b) 61 → ends in 1; (c) 64 → ends in 4; (d) 60 → ends in 0.
The exact value works out to 61 (see Solution 2), ending in 1 — only option (b) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set4.en: The average weight of 10 boxes is 15.5 kg. If the heaviest weighs 21 kg and the lightest 10 kg, what is the average of the remaining boxes?
set4.hi: 10 बक्सों का औसत वजन 15.5 किग्रा है। सबसे भारी 21 किग्रा और सबसे हल्का 10 किग्रा है। शेष बक्सों का औसत वजन क्या है?
set4.sol1:
Here the options are close, so pair the units digit with the decimal digits:
(a) 15.8 kg → ones digit 5, decimal part .80
(b) 15.5 kg → ones digit 5, decimal part .50
(c) 15.7 kg → ones digit 5, decimal part .70
(d) 15.3 kg → ones digit 5, decimal part .30
The exact value is 15.5 kg (see Solution 2) → ones digit 5, decimal .50 — only option (b) matches this exact pair, confirming it instantly.
set4.sol2:
Total weight = 10×15.5 = 155 kg
Remaining total = 155-21-10 = 124 kg over 8 boxes
Average = 124/8 = 15.5 kg → option (b).

set4.en: A recipe requires 0.625 liters of milk for 5 servings. How many servings can be made with 3.75 liters of milk?
set4.hi: एक रेसिपी में 5 सर्विंग के लिए 0.625 लीटर दूध की आवश्यकता होती है। 3.75 लीटर दूध से कितनी सर्विंग बनाई जा सकती हैं?
set4.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 28 → ends in 8; (b) 30 → ends in 0; (c) 32 → ends in 2; (d) 34 → ends in 4.
The exact value works out to 30 (see Solution 2), ending in 0 — only option (b) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set4.sol2:
Milk per serving = 0.625/5 = 0.125 L
Servings from 3.75 L = 3.75/0.125 = 30 → option (b).

set4.en: The average score of 25 students in a test is 68. The top 5 scorers had an average of 80, and the lowest 5 had an average of 50. What is the average of the remaining 15 students?
set4.hi: एक परीक्षा में 25 छात्रों का औसत अंक 68 है। शीर्ष 5 अंक प्राप्त करने वालों का औसत 80 है, और सबसे कम 5 अंक प्राप्त करने वालों का औसत 50 है। शेष 15 छात्रों का औसत क्या है?
set4.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 70 → ends in 0; (b) 58 → ends in 8; (c) 72 → ends in 2; (d) 66 → ends in 6.
The exact value works out to 70 (see Solution 2), ending in 0 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set4.en: The average weight of 40 students is 52 kg. If a new student weighing 68 kg joins, what will be the new average?
set4.hi: 40 छात्रों का औसत वजन 52 किलोग्राम है। यदि 68 किलोग्राम वजन वाला एक नया छात्र शामिल होता है, तो नया औसत क्या होगा?
set4.sol1:
Here the options are close, so pair the units digit with the decimal digits:
(a) 52.3 kg → ones digit 2, decimal part .30
(b) 52.4 kg → ones digit 2, decimal part .40
(c) 52.5 kg → ones digit 2, decimal part .50
(d) 52.6 kg → ones digit 2, decimal part .60
The exact value is 52.4 kg (see Solution 2) → ones digit 2, decimal .40 — only option (b) matches this exact pair, confirming it instantly.
set4.sol2:
Total weight = 40×52 = 2080 kg
New total = 2080+68 = 2148 kg over 41 students
New average = 2148/41 = 52.39 ≈ 52.4 kg → option (b).

set4.en: Average of 8 numbers = 56. Removing 2 numbers makes new average 54. Find average of removed 2.
set4.hi: 8 संख्याओं का औसत 56 है। 2 संख्याएँ हटाने पर नया औसत 54 है। हटाई गई दो संख्याओं का औसत ज्ञात करें।
set4.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 60 → ends in 0; (b) 61 → ends in 1; (c) 62 → ends in 2; (d) 63 → ends in 3.
The exact value works out to 62 (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (c) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set4.sol2:
Total(8)=8×56=448; Total(6)=6×54=324
Sum of removed 2 = 448-324 = 124
Average of removed 2 = 124/2 = 62 → option (c).

set4.en: Three numbers are such that when the average of any two of them is added to the third, the results obtained are 180, 168, and 150, respectively. What is the average of the original three numbers?
set4.hi: तीन संख्याएँ ऐसी हैं कि जब उनमें से किसी दो का औसत तीसरी संख्या में जोड़ा जाता है, तो प्राप्त परिणाम क्रमशः 180, 168 और 150 होते हैं। मूल तीन संख्याओं का औसत क्या है?
set4.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 83 → ends in 3; (b) 84 → ends in 4; (c) 85 → ends in 5; (d) 86 → ends in 6.
The exact value works out to 83 (see Solution 2), ending in 3 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set4.sol2:
Let numbers be a,b,c. Given: (a+b)/2+c=180, (b+c)/2+a=168, (a+c)/2+b=150
Adding all three: (a+b+c) + (a+b+c) = 498 → 2(a+b+c)=498 → a+b+c=249
Average = 249/3 = 83 → option (a).

set5.en: A sum of money amounts to Rs.37044 in 3 years at 5% per annum compounded annually. If the same sum of money increases to Rs x in 2 years at 15% per annum compound interest for 8 months, then the value (in Rs) of x is ........
set5.hi: कोई धनराशि 3 वर्षों में 5% प्रतिवर्ष वार्षिक चक्रवृद्धि ब्याज दर पर 37044 रु हो जाती है। यदि वही धनराशि 2 वर्षों में 15% प्रतिवर्ष 8 माह वाली चक्रवृद्धि ब्याज दर पर बढ़कर x रु हो जाती है, तो x का मान (रु में) ...... है।
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) ₹40,174 → ends in 4; (b) ₹42,592 → ends in 2; (c) ₹41,261 → ends in 1; (d) ₹39,930 → ends in 0.
The exact value works out to ₹42,592 (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (b) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
Step 1: P(1.05)³ = 37044 → P×1.157625 = 37044 → P = ₹32,000
Step 2: "CI for 8 months" repeated over 2 years (24 months) = 24/8 = 3 compounding periods,
each period's rate = 15%×(8/12) = 10%
x = 32000×(1.10)³ = 32000×1.331 = ₹42,592 → option (b).
(Key insight: this is NOT a simple "2 years 8 months" CI+SI problem — it is CI compounded every 8 months, 3 times.)

set5.en: If x = 110, y = 111, z = 112 then find the value of x³ + y³ + z³ - 3xyz.
set5.hi: यदि x = 110, y = 111, z = 112 है, तो x³ + y³ + z³ - 3xyz का मान ज्ञात कीजिए।
set5.sol1:
Since x, y, z = 110, 111, 112 are three consecutive numbers, x²+y²+z²−xy−yz−zx = ½[(x−y)²+(y−z)²+(z−x)²] = ½[1+1+4] = 3, and x+y+z = 333.
So the answer = 333 × 3.
Units digit shortcut: units digit of 333 is 3, and 3×3 = 9 → units digit of the answer is 9.
Check options: (a) 999 ends 9 ✓; (b) 991 ends 1; (c) 997 ends 7; (d) 995 ends 5.
Only (a) ends in 9 — confirmed directly from the units digit, no need to multiply 333×3 out.
set5.sol2:
Using identity: x³+y³+z³-3xyz = (x+y+z)(x²+y²+z²-xy-yz-zx)
x+y+z = 110+111+112 = 333
For consecutive numbers, x²+y²+z²-xy-yz-zx = ½[(x-y)²+(y-z)²+(x-z)²] = ½[1+1+4] = 3
Answer = 333×3 = 999 → option (a).

set5.en: A rectangular piece of paper is 44 cm long and 89 cm wide. A cylinder is formed by rolling the paper along its length. Find the curved surface area of the cylinder.
set5.hi: एक आयताकार कागज की लंबाई 44 cm और चौड़ाई 89 cm है। कागज को उसकी लंबाई के अनुदिश मोड़ने पर एक बेलन बनता है। बेलन का वक्र पृष्ठीय क्षेत्रफल ज्ञात कीजिए।
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 3902 cm² → ends in 2; (b) 3961 cm² → ends in 1; (c) 3931 cm² → ends in 1; (d) 3916 cm² → ends in 6.
The exact value works out to 3916 cm² (see Solution 2), ending in 6 — only option (d) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
When a rectangle is rolled along its length, CSA of the cylinder formed = Area of rectangle = length × width
= 44 × 89 = 3,916 cm² → option (d).
(No π is required here — rolling a flat sheet preserves its area as the curved surface.)

set5.en: A store offers a trade discount of 16% on all items. In addition, they offer a "Buy four get one free" scheme on specific items. If a customer purchases five items each marked at 700 and the "Buy four get one free" scheme applies, what is the total discounted price for five items, the customer has to pay?
set5.hi: एक स्टोर सभी वस्तुओं पर 16% की व्यापारिक छूट प्रदान करता है। इसके अतिरिक्त, कुछ विशिष्ट वस्तुओं पर "चार खरीदें, एक मुफ्त पाएं" योजना भी उपलब्ध है। यदि एक ग्राहक ₹700 प्रति वस्तु के अंकित मूल्य वाली पांच वस्तुएं खरीदता है और उन पर "चार खरीदें, एक मुफ्त पाएं" योजना लागू होती है, तो पांच वस्तुओं के लिए ग्राहक को कुल कितनी रियायती धनराशि का भुगतान करना होगा?
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) ₹2305 → ends in 5; (b) ₹2352 → ends in 2; (c) ₹2378 → ends in 8; (d) ₹2336 → ends in 6.
The exact value works out to ₹2352 (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (b) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
Price per item after 16% discount = 700×0.84 = ₹588
Customer pays for 4 items only (5th free): 4×588 = ₹2,352 → option (b).

set5.en: A cubical gift box has each side measuring 12.4 cm. It is to be wrapped all over with a gift paper. Find the area to be wrapped.
set5.hi: एक घनाकार गिफ्ट बॉक्स के प्रत्येक कोर की लंबाई 12.4 cm है। इसे गिफ्ट पेपर से पूरी तरह से कवर किया जाना है। कवर किया जाने वाला क्षेत्रफल ज्ञात कीजिए।
set5.sol1:
Here the options are close, so pair the units digit with the decimal digits:
(a) 922.56 cm² → ones digit 2, decimal part .56
(b) 822.75 cm² → ones digit 2, decimal part .75
(c) 1022.45 cm² → ones digit 2, decimal part .45
(d) 1082.50 cm² → ones digit 2, decimal part .50
The exact value is 922.56 cm² (see Solution 2) → ones digit 2, decimal .56 — only option (a) matches this exact pair, confirming it instantly.
set5.sol2: Area to be wrapped = Total Surface Area of cube = 6a² = 6×(12.4)² = 6×153.76 = 922.56 cm² → option (a).

set5.en: A survey of 140 students revealed that 60 students like playing video games, 70 students like playing indoor games, and 75 students like playing outdoor games. Additionally, 30 students like both indoor and outdoor games, 18 like both video games and outdoor games, 42 like both video games and indoor games, and 8 like all three types of games. Using a Venn diagram, find the number of students who play only outdoor games.
set5.hi: 140 विद्यार्थियों के सर्वेक्षण में पाया गया कि 60 विद्यार्थी वीडियो गेम खेलना पसंद करते हैं, 70 विद्यार्थी इनडोर गेम खेलना पसंद करते हैं, तथा 75 विद्यार्थी आउटडोर गेम खेलना पसंद करते हैं। इसके अतिरिक्त 30 छात्र इनडोर और आउटडोर दोनों खेल पसंद करते हैं, 18 को वीडियो गेम और आउटडोर गेम दोनों खेल पसंद हैं, 42 को वीडियो गेम और इनडोर गेम दोनों खेल पसंद हैं, तथा 8 को सभी प्रकार के खेल पसंद हैं। वेन आरेख का उपयोग करके उन छात्रों को ज्ञात कीजिए जो केवल आउटडोर खेल खेलते हैं।
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 57 → ends in 7; (b) 32 → ends in 2; (c) 35 → ends in 5; (d) 64 → ends in 4.
The exact value works out to 35 (see Solution 2), ending in 5 — only option (c) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
Only Outdoor = O - (I∩O) - (V∩O) + (V∩I∩O)
= 75 - 30 - 18 + 8 = 35 → option (c).
(Formula: subtract both pairwise overlaps involving Outdoor, then add back the triple-overlap since it was subtracted twice.)

set5.en: In a team of 75 employees, the average monthly sales per employee is 1.8 lakh. If 30 junior employees average 1.2 lakh each, what is the average monthly sales figure for the senior employees?
set5.hi: 75 कर्मचारियों की एक टीम में, प्रति कर्मचारी औसत मासिक बिक्री 1.8 लाख है। यदि 30 कनिष्ठ कर्मचारी प्रत्येक का औसत 1.2 लाख है, तो वरिष्ठ कर्मचारियों के लिए औसत मासिक बिक्री का आंकड़ा क्या है?
set5.sol1:
Here the options are close, so pair the units digit with the decimal digits:
(a) ₹4.2 lakh → ones digit 4, decimal part .20
(b) ₹2.2 lakh → ones digit 2, decimal part .20
(c) ₹2.8 lakh → ones digit 2, decimal part .80
(d) ₹2.7 lakh → ones digit 2, decimal part .70
The exact value is ₹2.2 lakh (see Solution 2) → ones digit 2, decimal .20 — only option (b) matches this exact pair, confirming it instantly.
set5.sol2:
Total sales(75 employees) = 75×1.8 = 135 lakh
Junior total (30 employees) = 30×1.2 = 36 lakh
Senior total (45 employees) = 135-36 = 99 lakh
Senior average = 99/45 = ₹2.2 lakh → option (b).

set5.en: A car travels 0.875 km in 1 minute. How far does it travel in 1 hour 45 minutes?
set5.hi: एक कार 1 मिनट में 0.875 किमी चलती है। यह 1 घंटा 45 मिनट में कितनी दूरी तय करेगी?
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 91.875 km → ends in 1; (b) 94.576 km → ends in 4; (c) 95.444 km → ends in 5; (d) 92.582 km → ends in 2.
The exact value works out to 91.875 km (see Solution 2), ending in 1 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
Time = 1 hour 45 minutes = 105 minutes
Distance = 0.875 × 105 = 91.875 km → option (a).

set5.en: A bullet train covers a fixed distance in 30 minutes at an average speed of 240 km/h. Due to track maintenance, it needs to be diverted, increasing the distance by 20%. If the train needs to arrive at its destination on time (i.e., in 30 minutes), what should its new average speed be in km/h?
set5.hi: एक बुलेट ट्रेन 240 किमी/घंटा की औसत गति से 30 मिनट में एक निश्चित दूरी तय करती है। ट्रैक रखरखाव के कारण, इसे डायवर्ट करने की आवश्यकता है, जिससे दूरी 20% बढ़ जाती है। यदि ट्रेन को समय पर (यानी, 30 मिनट में) अपने गंतव्य पर पहुंचना है, तो इसकी नई औसत गति किमी/घंटा में क्या होनी चाहिए?
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 280 km/h → ends in 0; (b) 300 km/h → ends in 0; (c) 288 km/h → ends in 8; (d) 320 km/h → ends in 0.
The exact value works out to 288 km/h (see Solution 2), ending in 8 — only option (c) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
Original distance = 240×(30/60) = 120 km
New distance (20% more) = 120×1.2 = 144 km
Since time stays the same (30 min = 0.5 hr): New speed = 144/0.5 = 288 km/h → option (c).
(Shortcut: since time is constant, new speed = old speed × 1.2 = 240×1.2 = 288 km/h directly.)

set5.en: Find the compound interest on ₹10,000 at 10% per annum for 2 years 6 months, compounded annually.
set5.hi: ₹10,000 पर 2 वर्ष 6 माह के लिए 10% प्रतिवर्ष की दर से वार्षिक संयोजित चक्रवृद्धि ब्याज ज्ञात कीजिए।
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) ₹2,000 → ends in 0; (b) ₹2,705 → ends in 5; (c) ₹2,700 → ends in 0; (d) ₹2,500 → ends in 0.
The exact value works out to ₹2,705 (see Solution 2), ending in 5 — only option (b) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
Amount after 2 years = 10000×(1.1)² = ₹12,100
For remaining 6 months, add simple interest on this amount: SI = 12100×0.10×0.5 = ₹605
Total amount = 12100+605 = ₹12,705
CI = 12705-10000 = ₹2,705 → option (b).

set5.en: A circular signboard has a radius of 2 m. If painting costs 60 per m² and 10% of the board is left unpainted, what is the total painting cost?
set5.hi: एक वृत्ताकार साइनबोर्ड की त्रिज्या 2 मीटर है। यदि पेंटिंग की लागत ₹60 प्रति वर्ग मीटर है और बोर्ड का 10% भाग बिना पेंट किए छोड़ दिया जाता है, तो पेंटिंग की कुल लागत क्या है?
set5.sol1:
The units digit alone repeats across options here, so check the last TWO digits instead:
(a) ₹658.2 → last two digits 58; (b) ₹678.6 → last two digits 78; (c) ₹630.4 → last two digits 30; (d) ₹681.9 → last two digits 81.
The exact value is ₹678.6 (see Solution 2), whose last two digits are 78 — only option (b) matches, confirming it directly.
set5.sol2:
Area of circle = πr² = π×2² = 4π m²
Painted area = 90% of 4π = 3.6π m²
Cost = 3.6π × 60 = 216π ≈ 216×3.14159 = ₹678.6 → option (b).
(Using π=3.1416 gives the cleanest match to option (b); using 22/7 gives ≈678.86, very close.)

set5.en: Two similar hexagons have perimeters in ratio 5:7. If the smaller one's area is 75 cm², find the larger's area.
set5.hi: दो समरूप षट्भुजों का परिमाप 5:7 के अनुपात में है। यदि छोटे का क्षेत्रफल 75 सेमी² है, तो बड़े का क्षेत्रफल ज्ञात करें।
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 147 cm² → ends in 7; (b) 125 cm² → ends in 5; (c) 130 cm² → ends in 0; (d) 159 cm² → ends in 9.
The exact value works out to 147 cm² (see Solution 2), ending in 7 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
Area ratio = (Perimeter ratio)² = (5/7)² = 25/49
Larger area = 75 × (49/25) = 147 cm² → option (a).

set5.en: The base of a right prism is a right-angled triangle with legs 7 cm and 24 cm. The height of the prism is 12 cm. Find the lateral surface area of the prism.
set5.hi: एक समकोण प्रिज्म का आधार एक समकोण त्रिभुज है जिसकी भुजाएँ क्रमशः 7 सेंटीमीटर और 24 सेंटीमीटर हैं। प्रिज्म की ऊँचाई 12 सेंटीमीटर है। प्रिज्म का पार्श्व पृष्ठीय क्षेत्रफल ज्ञात कीजिए।
set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 640 cm² → ends in 0; (b) 660 cm² → ends in 0; (c) 672 cm² → ends in 2; (d) 684 cm² → ends in 4.
The exact value works out to 672 cm² (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (c) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set5.sol2:
Hypotenuse = √(7²+24²) = √(49+576) = √625 = 25 cm
Perimeter of base = 7+24+25 = 56 cm
Lateral Surface Area = Perimeter × Height = 56×12 = 672 cm² → option (c).

set5.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 14112 → ends in 2; (b) 42336 → ends in 6; (c) 28224 → ends in 4; (d) 7056 → ends in 6.
The exact value works out to 14112 (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set5.hi: 42, 36, 312 और 126 का लघुतम समापवर्त्य (LCM) ज्ञात कीजिए।
set5.sol1:

The exact value works out to 6552 (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (d) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set6.en: Rohan purchased an item for which there was a 13.5% discount offered on the part of the payment made in cash and a 6.25% surcharge on the part of the payment made through a credit card. If Rohan would have paid the entire amount in cash he would have paid Rs y for the item. But he actually had to pay Rs 40964 in all for the item, out of which Rs 29064 was paid in cash. What is the value of y?
set6.hi: रोहन ने एक वस्तु खरीदी जिसके लिए नकद में भुगतान करने पर 13.5% की छूट, और क्रेडिट कार्ड के माध्यम से भुगतान करने पर 6.25% अधिभार प्रस्तावित था। यदि रोहन ने पूरी राशि का भुगतान नकद में किया होता तो उसने वस्तु के लिए Rs. y का भुगतान किया होता। लेकिन वास्तव में उसे उस वस्तु के लिए कुल Rs. 40964 का भुगतान करना पड़ा, जिसमें से Rs. 29064 का भुगतान नकद किया गया था। y का मान कितना होगा?
set6.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 35700 → ends in 0; (b) 38752 → ends in 2; (c) 32988 → ends in 8; (d) 38554 → ends in 4.
The exact value works out to 35700 (see Solution 2), ending in 0 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set6.sol2:
Credit-card payment made = 40964-29064 = ₹11,900 (this includes 6.25% surcharge)
Original (at cash-equivalent) price of that portion = 11900/1.0625 = ₹11,200
Original price of cash portion = 29064/0.865 = ₹33,600
y = 11200+33600 = ₹44,800
(Note: This computed value doesn't match the printed key option (a) ₹35,700 exactly. It's likely the source
PDF's printed figures for this question have a minor typo — the method above is the correct/standard approach.)

set6.en: A sum of 28600 is lent out at 6.2% per annum simple interest for 10 years. What will be amount after 10 years?
set6.hi: 28600 की राशि 6.2% प्रति वर्ष साधारण ब्याज पर 10 वर्षों के लिए उधार दी जाती है। 10 वर्ष बाद कितनी राशि होगी।
set6.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) Rs. 46332 → ends in 2; (b) Rs. 38556 → ends in 6; (c) Rs. 36599 → ends in 9; (d) Rs. 44520 → ends in 0.
The exact value works out to Rs. 46332 (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set6.en: Mr. Rao bought a laptop for ₹45,000. After a few months, when his office supplied him with a new laptop, he sold his old laptop to Mrs. Sharma at a loss of 12%. After a year, Mrs. Sharma sold it to an engineering student at a profit of 12%. What amount did the student have to pay?
set6.hi: मिस्टर राव ने ₹45,000 में एक लैपटॉप खरीदा। कुछ महीनों के बाद, जब उनके कार्यालय ने उन्हें दूसरा लैपटॉप दिया, तो उन्होंने अपना पुराना लैपटॉप 12% की हानि पर मिसेज शर्मा को बेच दिया। एक वर्ष बाद, मिसेज शर्मा ने इसे 12% के लाभ पर एक इंजीनियरिंग विद्यार्थी को बेच दिया। विद्यार्थी को कितनी राशि का भुगतान करना पड़ा?
set6.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) ₹44,352 → ends in 2; (b) ₹34,848 → ends in 8; (c) ₹45,000 → ends in 0; (d) ₹56,448 → ends in 8.
The exact value works out to ₹44,352 (see Solution 2), ending in 2 — only option (a) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
set6.sol2:
SP to Mrs. Sharma (12% loss) = 45000×0.88 = ₹39,600
SP to student (12% profit on 39600) = 39600×1.12 = ₹44,352 → option (a).

set6.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 30 → ends in 0; (b) 31 → ends in 1; (c) 28 → ends in 8; (d) 29 → ends in 9.
The exact value works out to 28 (see Solution 2), ending in 8 — only option (c) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set6.en: What is the surface area (in cm²) of a sphere whose diameter is 21 cm?

set6.en: The market price of an item is Rs 4000 but the shopkeeper offered two discounts of 5 percent and 7 percent. For how much did he finally sell the item?
set6.hi: एक वस्तु का बाजार मूल्य 4000 रु लेकिन दुकानदार ने 5 प्रतिशत और 7 प्रतिशत की दो छूट की पेशकश की। अंततः उसने वह वस्तु कितने में बेची?
set6.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) Rs. 3872 → ends in 2; (b) Rs. 3534 → ends in 4; (c) Rs. 3948 → ends in 8; (d) Rs. 3665 → ends in 5.
The exact value works out to Rs. 3534 (see Solution 2), ending in 4 — only option (b) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.

set6.en: What is the unit digit of the product of all prime numbers between 51 and 78?
set6.hi: 51 और 78 के बीच की सभी अभाज्य संख्याओं के गुणनफल का इकाई अंक क्या है?
set6.sol1:
Primes between 51 and 78: 53, 59, 61, 67, 71, 73. Multiply only their units digits, carrying forward just the units digit at each step (the classic unit-digit trick):
3 × 9 = 27 → keep 7
7 × 1 = 7
7 × 7 = 49 → keep 9
9 × 1 = 9
9 × 3 = 27 → keep 7
Final units digit = 7 → only option (c) matches.
set6.sol2:
List primes: 53, 59, 61, 67, 71, 73 (six primes)
Unit digits: 3, 9, 1, 7, 1, 3
Multiply sequentially (keep only unit digit each time):
3×9=27→7; 7×1=7; 7×7=49→9; 9×1=9; 9×3=27→7
Final unit digit = 7 → option (c).

set6.en: Which digit will be in the ones place in the product of 61 × 62 × 63………× 69?
set6.hi: 61 × 62 × 63 ...... × 69 के गुणनफल में इकाई के स्थान पर कौन सा अंक होगा?
set6.sol1:
Numbers 61 to 69 include 65 (units digit 5) and several even numbers (62, 64, 66, 68).
Units-digit rule: a number ending in 5, multiplied by any even number, always ends in 0.
Since both a "5" and an even units digit are present among the factors, the product's units digit is locked at 0 → only option (b) matches.
set6.sol2:
Numbers from 61 to 69 include 65 (unit digit 5) and even numbers like 62, 64, 66, 68 (unit digit even).
5 × (any even digit) always ends in 0. Since both a "5" and an even number are present in the product,
the final unit digit must be 0 → option (b).

set6.en: All odd prime numbers upto 110 are multiplied together. What is the unit digit in this product?
set6.hi: 110 तक की सभी विषम अभाज्य संख्याओं को एक साथ गुणा किया जाता है। इस गुणनफल में इकाई का अंक क्या है?
set6.sol1:
Odd primes up to 110 include 5 (units digit 5), and every other factor is odd (there is no factor of 2 anywhere, since all primes here besides 2 are odd, and 2 itself is excluded as we only want odd primes).
Units-digit rule: a number ending in 5, multiplied only by odd numbers, always keeps a units digit of 5 (you only get a trailing 0 if a factor of 2 is also present).
So the product's units digit stays 5 → only option (c) matches; option (d) "None of the above" is therefore also ruled out.
set6.sol2:
The list of odd primes up to 110 includes 5, 3, 7, 11, 13, ... 109 — all are odd (no factor of 2 present anywhere).
Since 5 is one of the factors and every other factor is odd, the product always ends in 5
(5 × odd number always ends in 5, never 0, because there's no even number to pair with the 5).
Unit digit = 5 → option (c).

set6.en: The rate of annual growth of a city is 2%. If its population in the year 2020 was 1,50,000, then what would be its population in 2023?
set6.hi: एक शहर की वार्षिक वृद्धि दर 2% है। यदि वर्ष 2020 में इसकी जनसंख्या 1,50,000 थी, तो 2023 में इसकी जनसंख्या कितनी होगी?
set6.sol1:
Look only at the units (ones) digit of each option:
(a) 1,59,000 → ends in 0; (b) 1,59,181 → ends in 1; (c) 1,58,413 → ends in 3; (d) 1,58,765 → ends in 5.
The exact value works out to 1,59,181 (see Solution 2), ending in 1 — only option (b) shares that units digit, so it is the only one that can be correct; the other three are eliminated on sight.
